rabbit-on-the-hunt-teleculinaria.name: Rabbit On The Hunt
instructions: Fix and cut the rabbit into pieces, throw them in a bowl, add the red wine, onion and garlic cut into slices, season with salt, pepper, cloves and laurel leaf. Let it marinate for two hours.

Spill the rabbit pieces and reserve the marinade. Sprinkle the rabbit with the flour and wrap it. Take a pot to the fire with the olive oil, let it warm, add the rabbit and let it shine on both sides. Add onions, garlic and marinade blonde.

Add the tomato previously peeled and cut into cubes, boil a little, add the liquid from the marinade and refine spices. Then add the mushrooms previously arranged and cut into rooms, stir, tape and let cook slowly until everything becomes soft. Serve tastefully decorated with baked potatoes or other garrison.

raspberry-jam.name: Raspberry Jam
instructions: Place a small plate in the freezer. Bring raspberries, sugar, orange zest, orange juice, and lemon juice to a simmer in a large saucepan, stirring gently to dissolve sugar. Once sugar is dissolved, increase heat and boil, skimming foam from surface often, until mixture is reduced by a little more than a third and bottom of pot is visible when stirring, 15–20 minutes.

Dab a tablespoonful of jam onto frozen plate and freeze 1 minute. Drag a finger through jam to see if it wrinkles. If it does, remove saucepan from heat and stir in bitters. If jam doesn’t wrinkle, keep cooking, testing again every minute until it is does. Transfer jam to jar, cover, and let cool. Chill until ready to use.
Do Ahead: Jam can be made 2 months ahead. Keep chilled.

raw-fermented-beets.name: Raw Fermented Beets
instructions: Distribute beet chunks between two jars with lids. Add 2 cloves garlic, 3 peppercorns, and 1 bay leaf to each jar.
Combine water and salt in a bowl to make the brine; stir until salt is completely dissolved. Pour enough brine into the jars to completely cover the beets.

Screw on lids and let jars sit at room temperature until foam starts to appear on top, about 1 week. Transfer jars to a cool place (10°C, 10 degrees C) for 2 to 3 days after foam appears. Store in the refrigerator.

razors-with-herb-sauce-teleculinaria.name: Razors With Herb Sauce

Cut the purple cabbage into thin strips, lay them down for a bowl and water with vinegar. Peel the grapefruit and orange, carefully remove the buds with a sharp knife, in order to eliminate their skin, enjoying the juice that they release.
Drain very well with your hands the cabbage for broth, add to the purple cabbage, season with salt and pepper, add the chopped chives, water with oil and gently mix.

red-chilaquile-egg-bake.name: Red Chilaquile Egg Bake

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper

ingredients.prep: crumbled queso fresco, and chopped cilantro, for serving

instructions: Finely chop the white onion.
Peel and mince the garlic. Finely dice one jalapeno and thinly slice the remaining jalapeno, membrane removed.

Squeeze the lime until you have 2 tablespoons juice. Heat oil in a 10-inch, heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-high. Add onion and cook stirring occasionally, until lightly charred in places, about 4 minutes. Reduce heat to medium and add garlic and the finely diced jalapeno. Cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour the Campbell’s® Condensed Tomato Soup. Add in diced tomatoes, ½ cup water, lime juice, and chipotle chili sauce into the skillet with the onion mixture.
Bring to a boil, stirring to combine salt and pepper.
Stir in the tortilla chips until coated in sauce. Add eggs.
Bake until egg whites are set, about 13 minutes. Top with avocado, jalapenos, queso fresco, and cilantro.

red-velvet-cake-or-red-velvet-cake-peque.name: Red Velvet Cake Or Red Velvet Cake
red-velvet-cake-or-red-velvet-cake-peque.original_name: Red Velvet Cake o Tarta de Terciopelo Rojo

ingredients.item: 1 ½ tablespoon cocoa powder without sugar
190 ml Buttermilk at room temperature (3 / 4 cup whole milk + 3 / 4 tablespoon lemon juice)
2 ½ teaspoons of dyed red food gel
120 g de mantequilla sin sal blanda
2 cucharaditas de vinagre de sidra de manzana
125 g de mantequilla sin sal a temperatura ambiente
250 g de queso crema tipo philadelphia a temperatura ambiente

instructions: If you can't find Buttermilk, you can get them home: In a pitcher we mix 3 / 4 cup of whole milk and 3 / 4 tablespoons of lemon juice and let them rest 30 minutes at room temperature.

We tanked the flour with the cocoa powder and half a teaspoon of salt into a bowl. We'll have to figure something out. In another bowl, we add the Buttermilk and the Red Gel Color and mix well until the color is homogenous. Let's have a reservation.
In a bowl with a stick mixer, we beat butter with sugar and vanilla extract, till we get a clear and sparkling cream.
We add both eggs at room temperature and continue to bast for about 5 minutes till everything's well integrated.

We added the steamed flour with cacao little by little, alternating with the mixture of buttermilk and red dyes, and mixed well with each addition but without excessive beating because the biscuit would come out very dense. In a small bowl we mix 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar and 1 tablespoon of sodium bicarbonate and we immediately turn onto the mass, combining with a spatula with wrapped movements.
In a small bowl we mix 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar and 1 tablespoon of sodium bicarbonate and we immediately turn onto the mass, combining with a spatula with wrapped movements.

We divide the dough into three and distribute them into three mods for a lake of 15-18 cm (of this type), previously ground and pitted. We also advise you to put some oven paper at the base of the mold to avoid its gluing. If you have no 3 mold do it in several pits or do it in a higher and more straight mold and cut it into layers. With the pressurized oven we baked at 180 ° C heat up and down for about 20 minutes, depending on your oven. To figure out if they're made we'll have a stick and if it goes dry we can get them out. We wait about 5 minutes and then we turn them onto a grid and let them cool down.

While the biscuits are cold, we're preparing the cheese cover. We have been bawling with the stick mixer the butter next to the cheese for 2 minutes till the mixture is fluffy and homogenous. We added the sugar glas and the vanilla extract, and we continue to bast with the rods until we get a spongy and homogenous cream with no groove about 5 minutes.

We place the first biscuit at the foot of cake and put the first layer of stuffed we share well with a pastry scatula (as this) and put a new biscuit onto the pilling and we press slightly and we note that it's aligned with the lower biscuit. When working with pits in layers, the ideal is to level the upper parts (with this Utensil). The pitcher that you remove will not throw it out because then we can serve to decorate our Red Velvet cake with small little pitcher crumbs that will give them a lot of color: Once again we add two tablespoons of pilling, we distribute uniformly across the base, if it pits a little onto the sides nothing happens.

Finally we add the last biscuit and distribute the cover well onto the sides and the surface, we do not have to have the cover as perfect. We'll get them to the refrigerator till we get them to serve.
red-velvet-cake-or-red-velvet-cake-peque.category: Easy Beer Recipes, Home Stretch Recipes

red-wine-braised-brisket.name: Red Wine-braised Brisket
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Season brisket with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large ovenproof pot over medium-high. Cook brisket, turning occasionally, until browned all over, 8–10 minutes; transfer to a plate. Pour off fat from pot; discard.

Place onions, celery, garlic, thyme, bay leaves, tomatoes, tomato paste, and wine in pot and stir to combine; season with salt and pepper. Place brisket on top, fat side up. Cover and braise in oven, spooning juices, onions, and tomatoes over brisket every 30 minutes, until meat is fork-tender, 3–3 1/2 hours.

Uncover pot, nestle carrots around brisket, and cook until carrots are tender, top of brisket is browned and crisp, and sauce has thickened, about 30 minutes. Skim fat from surface of sauce; discard. Remove brisket from pot and slice against the grain to serve.

If not serving immediately, transfer brisket to a large bowl and pour braising liquid over; let cool in sauce. Cover and chill, at least 4 hours and up to 4 days. To serve, preheat oven to 325°. Skim fat from surface of sauce; discard. Cover and reheat brisket in sauce, 1–1 1/2 hours.

refrigerator-dessert-with-cookies-argiro.name: Refrigerator Dessert With Cookies
instructions: Beat the herbal cream with hand mixer for a few minutes until soft peaks are formed.
Add the praline and beat at low speed for 1 minute until the cream is homogenized.
Pour the liqueur into the milk and mix.
Break cookies into thick pieces.
In a 30×25 or nearby baking pan, we make one layer of brown cookies.
We sprinkle them with half the chocolate milk.
Spread half the cream.
Repeat layer with the remaining broken biscuits and pour them with the rest of the milk.
We make a layer of the rest of the cream.
Sprinkle the surface with hazelnuts mixed with chocolate zest.
Freeze for 4 to 5 hours or even better for one night.
We serve and enjoy.

reindeer-poop.name: Reindeer Poop
instructions: Place the white candy coating, German sweet chocolate, chocolate chips, and peanuts into a slow cooker.
Set the cooker to Low, cover, and gently heat the candy for 1 1/2 hours without stirring.
After 1 1/2 hours, stir the mixture, and spoon out by teaspoons onto waxed paper.
Allow to cool and set, about 45 minutes.

rice-with-chicken-and-vegetables-family-.name: Rice With Chicken And Vegetables ( Family Recipe)
rice-with-chicken-and-vegetables-family-.original_name: Arroz con pollo y verduras (receta familiar fácil)
instructions: We peel, wash and mince garlic teeth, carrots, green beans and peppers. We leapt them into the pan with some extra virgin olive oil.
When they start to get golden, we add the chicken cut into pieces and let them cook.

We add the rice and spices, we leap well and we add the chicken stock. When it starts to boil, we lower our fire and cook with medium heat about 20 minutes. We shut down, let's get some cover and serve.

rice-with-mushrooms-creamy-and-light-peq.name: Rice With Mushrooms, Creamy And Light
rice-with-mushrooms-creamy-and-light-peq.original_name: Arroz con setas, cremoso y ligero

ingredients.item: 2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra

instructions: Let's put the oil into a big frying pan and scattering the onion and garlic. We add mushrooms and we leap for 4 minutes, we put the wine back and let it evaporate the alcohol.

Once the alcohol has evaporated, we have put in the rice and we have been pounding for a few minutes and we have been stirring. We add milk and remove constantly, till the rice absorbs milk. Let's add the broth little by little and stir carefully, till the rice has absorbed all the broth. If necessary, add some more cauldron and season. Before we serve the rice, we sprinkle with a little parsley.

roast-chard-lezzet.name: Roast Chard
instructions: 1. Clean and wash away and chop the tears.
2. Clean the peppers and chop it in the form of membranes.
3. Clean onions and garlic and shore.
4. Heat the olive oil in the pan and roast the onion and garlic together with sugar.
5. Add the lube and flaws and roast 1-2 minutes.
6. Add salt and spices and taste.
7. Cook for 15 minutes in medium fire.
8. Add corns to five minutes to download from the stove.
9. Leave it from January and cool.
10. Take the service plate and serve cold.

roast-chicken-and-kimchi-smashed-potatoe.name: Roast Chicken And Kimchi Smashed Potatoes

ingredients.item: Fingerling or baby yukon gold potatoes

instructions: {'level': '1', 'description': 'Preheat oven to 450°. Toss potatoes and 1 tablespoon oil on a large rimmed baking sheet; season with salt and pepper. Roast, turning once, until browned in spots, 10-15 minutes.'}

{'level': '2', 'description': 'Meanwhile, rub chicken with garlic and season with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken skin side down until golden brown and crisp, 8-10 minutes.'}
{'level': '3', 'description': 'Arrange chicken skin side up on baking sheet among potatoes. Roast until chicken is cooked through and potatoes are tender, 15-20 minutes longer.'}
{'level': '4', 'description': 'Using a large spoon, lightly smash potatoes. Scatter kimchi over; roast until kimchi is warm, about 5 minutes.'}
{'level': '5', 'description': 'Meanwhile, whisk reserved kimchi liquid, vinegar, and 2 tablespoons oil in a small bowl.'}
{'level': '6', 'description': 'Scatter greens over, drizzle with half of dressing, and toss gently. Divide among plates and drizzle with remaining dressing.'}

roast-lamb-shoulder.name: Roast Lamb Shoulder
instructions: Lightly score fat on lamb in a crisscross pattern at 1" intervals. Place lamb in a large roasting pan and season generously with salt and pepper, coating evenly on all sides. Sprinkle garlic and rosemary all over lamb and drizzle with oil and lemon juice.

Using your hands, rub marinade into meat, making sure it’s completely coated. Arrange fat side up in pan. Cover very tightly with foil and chill for at least 2 hours or, preferably, overnight (the lamb will develop more flavor the longer it marinates).

Preheat oven to 325°. Roast lamb, basting occasionally and adding water by 1/4-cupfuls if pan is dry, until meat is completely tender and pulling easily away from the bones, 4–4 1/2 hours (be sure to secure foil tightly around the roasting pan each time you finish basting the lamb).
Remove lamb from oven and uncover. Preheat broiler. Broil lamb, basting occasionally, until fat is golden brown and crisp, 5–7 minutes.
Transfer lamb to a serving platter. Spoon fat from surface of juices in pan; discard fat. Pour juices into a pitcher and serve alongside lamb.

roasted-cauliflower-with-kalamata-vinaig.name: Roasted Cauliflower With Kalamata Vinaigrette
instructions: Preheat oven to 232°C with rack in lower third.

Cut cauliflower lengthwise into 3/4-inch-thick slices. Put in a large 4-sided sheet pan and toss with 2 tablespoon oil and 1/2 teaspoon each of salt and pepper. Roast, turning once or twice, until golden and just tender, about 25 minutes.

While cauliflower roasts, mince and mash garlic to a paste with a pinch of salt, then whisk together with lemon juice, remaining 2 tablespoons oil, olives, 1/8 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper. Serve cauliflower drizzled with Kalamata vinaigrette.

roasted-garlic-pull-apart-cheese-bread.name: Roasted Garlic Pull-apart Cheese Bread
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C.

Place the garlic cloves on a square of heavy foil, drizzle the olive oil over them, and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Wrap it up into a parcel and roast for 1 hour or until the garlic is golden, nutty, and soft. Set it aside to cool and reduce the oven temperature to 177°C. (You can roast the garlic up to a couple of days ahead of time and keep it in a small jar of oil in the fridge. Roast a bunch of cloves! You never know when you’ll need 'em.)

In a bowl, combine the cheeses, roasted garlic, chives, red pepper flakes, and some black pepper. Stir to mix everything together, smushing the garlic cloves so that little bits are distributed throughout the cheese. (You can get in there and use your fingers if you’d rather!)

Cut the bread in 1 1/2-inch-wide strips in one direction, taking care not to cut all the way through the loaf. Rotate the bread 90 degrees and cut the bread crosswise again. Move slowly and use caution! A serrated knife helps immensely.  Ta-da!

Set the bread on a large piece of foil and stuff the cheese mixture in between the rows and keep going until that bread is so stuffed with cheese, it can’t see straight! Drizzle the melted butter evenly all over the surface of the bread. Wrap the bread in foil and bake for 25 minutes. Open up the foil and bake it for another 10 minutes.
Carefully unwrap the bread and dig right in!

Make a pizza version:
Add chopped pepperoni, diced green bell pepper, and spoonfuls of marinara sauce.

How about a French onion version?
Diced caramelized onions, Gruyère cheese, and chopped chives. Drizzle on beef broth instead of butter. Yum!

My friend Meseidy has made a Buffalo chicken version:
Add mozzarella and a bunch of shredded chicken simmered in Louisiana hot sauce. Barbecue chicken would also be dreamy.

roasted-lemon-garlic-potato-wedges.name: Roasted Lemon-garlic Potato Wedges

ingredients.item: Freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.

Toss potato wedges with olive oil, melted butter, lemon juice, minced garlic, thyme, garlic salt, and onion powder in a large bowl. Season with freshly ground cracked pepper to taste. Spread the potatoes in an even layer in the prepared baking dish.

Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Remove the pan from the oven and flip the potato wedges. Continue roasting until the potatoes are golden and cooked through, about 20 minutes more. Sprinkle with parsley and serve.

roasted-mutton-argiro.name: Roasted Mutton

ingredients.item: Plenty of dry myzithra grated for serving
Plenty of dry myzithra grated for serving

instructions: We choose ewe (hand) and cut it into small portions.
Place it in a pot and cover with cold water.
Allow to boil for 5-6 minutes and strain. Throw away the first water.
Rinse the ewe to remove any bones and foams from the boil.
Wash the pot and put the portions back in.
Cover with plenty of water and let it boil.
As soon as the boil starts, lower the heat to medium and skim whenever foam appears on the surface.
Simmer until it softens for about 11⁄2 hours.
Take the rations off a platter and cover.
We drain the broth and hold it for later.

roasted-poblano-and-caper-salsa.name: Roasted Poblano And Caper Salsa

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Heat broiler. Broil chile on a broilerproof baking sheet, turning occasionally, until blackened in spots and blistered all over, 5–7 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let sit 10 minutes. Peel, seed, and coarsely chop chile; place in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a small skillet over medium and cook capers and cumin seeds, stirring, until mixture is fragrant and capers are crisp, about 30 seconds.
Remove peel and white pith from lemon; slice into thin rounds and remove seeds. Add to bowl with chile along with onion, parsley, and caper mixture; season with salt and pepper.

roasted-portabello-mushrooms-with-blue-c.name: Roasted Portabello Mushrooms With Blue Cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).
Place mushroom caps, tops facing down, on a baking sheet. Drizzle balsamic vinegar on each cap and sprinkle black pepper over vinegar.
Bake in the preheated oven until tender and fragrant, about 25 minutes. Sprinkle blue cheese, pine nuts, and garlic on each cap. Continue roasting until cheese melts, about 10 minutes more.

roasted-striped-bass-with-fennel-tomatoe.name: Roasted Striped Bass With Fennel, Tomatoes, And Oil-cured Olives

ingredients.item: Fresh fennel bulbs with fronds attached
ingredients.prep: trimmed; bulbs quartered lengthwise, then thinly sliced; fronds chopped and reserved for garnish
halved lengthwise through root end, thinly sliced (about 3 cups)

instructions: Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 204°C. Boil wine in medium saucepan until reduced to 1 cup, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and reserve.

Generously brush 18x12x1-inch baking sheet with olive oil. Arrange fennel slices in single layer on prepared baking sheet. Top with onion slices in single layer. Sprinkle with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Drizzle 4 tablespoons oil over vegetables.

Rinse fish inside and out; pat dry with paper towels. Sprinkle fish inside and out with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Lightly dust outside of fish with flour. Pour enough olive oil into extra-large skillet to reach depth of 1/4 inch; heat over medium-high heat until oil is very hot. Working with 1 fish at a time, add fish to skillet and fry until golden crust forms on skin, about 3 minutes per side. Carefully place fish atop vegetables on baking sheet. Gently stuff cavity of each fish with 2 crushed garlic cloves, then 1/4 cup chopped parsley. Pour reserved wine over vegetables on baking sheet.

Roast fish uncovered until vegetables begin to soften, 35 to 40 minutes. Scatter tomato halves and olives around fish; bake until fish is just cooked through, about 15 minutes longer. Transfer fish to large platter; cover with foil to keep warm.
Increase oven temperature to 246°C. Continue to bake vegetables uncovered until tender and tomatoes are very soft and beginning to color in spots, about 15 minutes longer.
Arrange vegetable mixture around fish on platter. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons oil. Sprinkle chopped fennel fronds over fish and serve.

An Italian Primitivo (a red varietal similar to Zinfandel) would be great with the fish and the pasta. Donatella prefers Primitivo di Manduria, a fruity, spicy style of red produced in Manduria, which is a city (as well as an area) in southern Puglia. She suggests the 2007 Feudi di San Gregorio 'Ognissole' Primitivo di Manduria (Italy, $18), a balanced wine with aromas of cherries and strawberries and a spicy finish. If you can't find that specific bottle, look for the 2007 Vinosia Salento Primitivo (Italy, $10), a juicy, fragrant red wine with honey notes.

roasted-sweet-potatoes-with-speck-and-ch.name: Roasted Sweet Potatoes With Speck And Chimichurri

ingredients.item: Fresh cilantro leaves with tender stems
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Heat oven to 425°. Toss sweet potatoes and 2 Tbsp. oil on a large rimmed baking sheet; season with salt and pepper. Roast, turning once, until tender, 25–30 minutes.

Meanwhile, pulse cilantro, parsley, oregano, thyme, and garlic in a food processor until finely chopped. With motor running, slowly add vinegar and remaining ¼ cup oil and process until combined; season with salt and pepper.
Spoon chimichurri onto a serving platter and top with sweet potatoes and Speck.

roman-holiday-cocktail.name: Roman Holiday Cocktail
instructions: Muddle mint leaves and simple syrup in a cocktail shaker. Add amaro, vermouth, and lemon juice; fill shaker with ice. Shake until outside of shaker is frosty, about 30 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass filled with ice and garnish with mint sprig and lemon wedge.

ruffled-milk-pie-with-raspberries.name: Ruffled Milk Pie With Raspberries
instructions: Heat oven to 400°. Butter an 8"-diameter or 8x8" baking dish. Scatter one third of raspberries over bottom of dish.

Place phyllo on a surface; cover with a barely damp kitchen towel. Working with 1 sheet, brush butter over 1 side. Carefully scrunch up to bring long sides together to make a rope, then wrap into a coil. Try not to smash pastry down; you want to keep it tall and somewhat loose. Place in center of prepared pan. Repeat with remaining phyllo and butter, placing coils around the one in the center. Scatter remaining berries over and sprinkle with 1 Tbsp. (13 g) granulated sugar.
Bake until pastry is golden brown and crisp, about 20 minutes. Remove from oven; reduce oven temperature to 350°.

Whisk eggs, egg yolks, milk, cream, vanilla, cinnamon, and remaining ½ cup (100 g) granulated sugar in a large bowl to combine. Pour custard over pastry and bake until custard is set, 20–30 minutes. Serve pie warm or at room temperature, dusted with powdered sugar.

rugelach.name: Rugelach
instructions: {'level': '1', 'description': 'Whisk together flour and salt in a bowl.'}
{'level': '2', 'description': 'Beat together butter and cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until combined well.'}
{'level': '3', 'description': 'Add flour mixture and stir with a wooden spoon until a soft dough forms.'}
{'level': '4', 'description': 'Gather dough into a ball and wrap in plastic wrap, then flatten (in wrap) into a roughly 7x5 rectangle.'}
{'level': '5', 'description': 'Chill until firm, 8 to 24 hours.'}
{'level': '6', 'description': 'Put oven rack in middle position and preheat oven to 180\\u00b0C.'}
{'level': '7', 'description': 'Line bottom of 1–1.5"-deep large shallow baking pan with parchment paper.'}
{'level': '8', 'description': 'Cut dough into 4 pieces.'}
{'level': '9', 'description': 'Chill 3 pieces, wrapped in plastic wrap, and roll out remaining piece into a 12x8 rectangle on a well-floured surface.'}
{'level': '10', 'description': 'Transfer dough to a sheet of parchment, then transfer to a tray and chill while rolling out remaining dough in same manner,'}
{'level': '11', 'description': 'Arrange 1 dough rectangle on work surface with a long side nearest you.'}
{'level': '12', 'description': 'Spread 60g preserves evenly over dough with offset spatula.'}
{'level': '13', 'description': 'Sprinkle 60g raisins and a rounded 30g walnuts over jam,'}
{'level': '14', 'description': 'Using parchment as an aid, roll up dough tightly into a log.'}
{'level': '15', 'description': 'Place, seam side down, in lined baking pan, then pinch ends closed and tuck underneath.'}
{'level': '16', 'description': 'Make 3 more logs in same manner and arrange 1" apart in pan.'}
{'level': '17', 'description': 'Brush logs with milk and sprinkle each with 1 tsp. of remaining granulated sugar.'}
{'level': '18', 'description': 'With a sharp large knife, make ¾"-deep cuts crosswise in dough (not all the way through) at 1" intervals.'}
{'level': '19', 'description': 'Bake until golden, 45 to 50 minutes.'}
{'level': '20', 'description': 'Cool to warm on baking sheet set atop wire rack,'}
